location: Very rare (0.5%) drop from the two gold-armored Wandering Nobles — wearing the Aristocrat Hat and Aristocrat Coat — found just north of the Mistwood Outskirts Site of Grace in eastern Limgrave, following a torch-bearing servant. Plain Wandering Nobles found elsewhere in the game do not carry this weapon.

source.name: Wandering Noble (Aristocrat-garbed pair, north of Mistwood Outskirts)

tactics: From Mistwood Outskirts grace, head north to find the gold-armored pair trailing a torch-bearing servant — they're the only Wandering Nobles that can drop this weapon
Wandering Nobles are unarmored and go down in a couple of hits, so the grind is entirely about drop luck, not difficulty
Their attacks are slow and easy to block or dodge; feel free to fight both at once rather than trying to isolate them
0.5% is a genuinely long farm — item discovery boosts (Silver-Tear Mask, Gold Scarab talisman, Silver Scarab talisman) meaningfully cut down the reset count
lore: A lighter, easier-to-handle estoc favored by wandering nobles, gilded from hilt to point. It's as much a status symbol as a weapon, and would fetch a tidy sum if sold.

builds.name: Early Quality pick
builds.why: Its base scaling is an exactly even STR/DEX split, and it carries the lowest Dexterity requirement of any thrusting sword, making it usable as a cheap Quality-build weapon from very early in the game.

builds.name: Budget status proc
builds.why: Blood or Poison affinity bolts on a solid status buildup while keeping the even Str/Dex scaling largely intact, useful as a low-level status weapon before better options open up.
